title: Qualys Scan Management API - Launch, Monitor, And Retrieve Scan Results
applies_to: Triggering Qualys VMDR scans programmatically from PowerShell or Logic Apps
Monitoring scan job status and waiting for completion before extracting detections
Selecting scanner appliances and option profiles for targeted scans

confirmed_facts.fact: Launch a scan: `POST https://{qualysBase}/api/2.0/fo/scan/?action=launch` with form-encoded body: `scan_title=MyAutomatedScan&ip=192.168.1.0/24&option_title=Initial+Options&iscanner_name=ScannerAppliance1`. Returns XML with `<VALUE>job_ref</VALUE>` — the scan reference ID used to track the job. Required headers: Authorization (Basic), X-Requested-With, Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded.

Launch scan targeting asset tags: `target_from=tags&use_ip_nt_range_tags=0&tag_include_selector=all&tag_set_by=id&tag_set_include=12345,67890`. This is preferred over IP-based targeting for environments where IPs change or assets are grouped by function (e.g., tag 'Production Servers', tag 'DMZ').

List scan jobs and check status: `GET https://{qualysBase}/api/2.0/fo/scan/?action=list&scan_ref={scanRef}`. Returns XML with STATUS/STATE element: `Running`, `Finished`, `Canceled`, `Error`. Poll this endpoint until STATE is `Finished` before calling the detection API. Recommended polling interval: 60 seconds.

Retrieve scan raw results (XML report): `GET https://{qualysBase}/api/2.0/fo/scan/?action=fetch&scan_ref={scanRef}&mode=brief`. Returns XML scan report. `mode=brief` returns a summary; `mode=extended` returns full detection details per host. Use this for storing scan evidence; use the detection API for structured data extraction.

List available option profiles: `GET https://{qualysBase}/api/2.0/fo/subscription/option_profile/?action=list`. Returns all option profiles defined in the subscription. Option profile name or ID is required when launching scans. Pre-validate the profile exists before embedding it in automation scripts.

List scanner appliances: `GET https://{qualysBase}/api/2.0/fo/appliance/?action=list`. Returns each scanner's `ID`, `NAME`, `STATUS` (Online/Offline), `RUNNING_SLICES_COUNT`. Use scanner name in scan launch calls. Always verify scanner status is Online before launching. External Scanner (Qualys cloud scanner) can be used for internet-facing assets without a local appliance.

implementation_notes: Wrap scan launch and wait in a PowerShell function: launch scan, capture scan_ref, then loop polling the status API every 60 seconds until STATE is 'Finished' or a timeout (e.g., 4 hours) is reached. After 'Finished', call the detection API filtered by `scan_ref` to get this scan's detections specifically.
Use tag-based targeting rather than IP ranges for production automation. Tags persist across IP changes and are easier to maintain as the environment scales.

For the daily reporting pipeline, scans are typically pre-scheduled in the Qualys portal and the PowerShell script only needs to extract detections. Only trigger scans programmatically if on-demand scanning is a specific requirement.
caveats: Scan launching requires API access enabled in the Qualys subscription and the API user to have 'Launch Scans' permission in their role.
Concurrent scan limits depend on the Qualys subscription tier. Launching too many scans simultaneously may return an error or queue the scan. Check scanner availability before launching.
The scan_ref is a session-scoped identifier. After the scan is purged from Qualys history (retention policy), the reference is no longer valid for status queries.
